sutta: the buddha said monks these 4 who are accomplished in wisdom disciplined confident deeply learned dharma bearers who live according to dhamma these 4 illuminate the order sangha which 4 among who is accomplished in wisdom disciplined confidence deeply learned dharma bearer who lives according to dhamma eliminates the order osanga a nun who is accomplished in wisdom disciplined confidence etc illuminates the order so also a male lay disciple who is accomplished in wisdom discipline confident deeply learned etc and the fourth 1 is a female lay disciple who is accomplished in wisdom discipline confident deeply learned etc these are the 4 who being accomplished in wisdom disciplined confident deeply learned dharma bearers living in accordance with dharma illuminate the order this is 1 suta where we find that the sangha refers not only to monks and nuns but also to male lay disciples and female disciples this sometimes is called the fourfold assembly so for example when we take a refuge in the sangha sometimes as you have listened earlier sometimes when we when we take refuge with the sangha we find that it refers to the bhikkhu sangha as we have heard some of the earlier sutas where the lay people are in the presence of the buddha they say they take refuge in the buddha the dhamma and the bhikkhu sangha and sometimes the sangha refers to this fourfold assembly and is also explained as the 8 areas 8 types of alias next 2 times
